rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VALERIE E. SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] 3) Please provide evidence showing the Fidelity IRA had Alfred Smith as the POD beneficiary. 4) Please file timesheets for the conservator of the estate. [LRSF 14.52]. 5) Please provide court order or explain and provide invoices for entries on disbursements 08/04/2020 Kato, Feder & Suzki legal fees for $474.70 and 08/04/2020 for $103.00. 6) Conservatee died 04/09/2021. Please explain the disbursements to Janet Jean Motika on 06/04/2021 and 06/07/2021 for music therapy $3,288.75 and $2,520.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/27/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE MATTER CAMACLANG FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. contrary to allegations in the petition, the real property at issue was not on schedule A; there is no signed comprehensive transfer document, and no assignment of real estate to revocable living trust. therefore, these facts do not fall within Heggstad and Counsel may be asked to provide a legal basis for this court to confirm the after-acquired property as a trust asset, given the need for a writing. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must file proof of publication for the amended petition. [CRC 7.53, Probate Code 8120 et seq]. 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAKO U ROVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/3/22. Still no response as of 5/27/22 to issues posted in the tentative ruling for 3/2/22. If issues are not addressed in time to deliver courtesy copies of the responsive papers and a proposed order to the assigned examiner by 7/25/22, an appearance may be required on 8/3/22 for the Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WAYNE ANDERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/11/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] If not all heirs waive bond, bond must be for the full estimated value of the estate. (Alternatively, bond could be just for the value of personal property if only limited IAEA authority were sought/granted.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/1/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VERA WALKER BURNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: hearing. apperance required as petition seeks to admit a lost will. counsel may be asked to clarify the statement that the presumption of revocation should not apply, even though the will was in the possession of the decedent at the time of her death and they do not allege she lacked capacity. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD J. GIETZEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/20/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7, and 14.59 E.] A cursory review of the papers online indicates that the petition is incomplete in its caption and the telephone number is missing from the Acknowledgement of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Richard Rogers is identified in the will as a cousin, but item 8 of the petition does not indicate his relationship to the decedent. Who were his parent who was the sibling of Decedent's parent and who were the common grandparents? If the whereabouts of a person are unknown, a declaration must be filed describing the efforts made to locate the person. [CRC 7.52(a).] This Court requires an ex parte petition to dispense with notice to such person if, after diligent search, the person cannot be located. Item 8 of the petition is missing the alternate executor (who must also be given notice) and also does not indicate who the trustee of the devisee trust is. If the petitioner is the trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust must be identified and must receive notice. If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately, conventionally, and confidentially. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/11/22 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
